title: Tax-Efficient Investing Strategies - Client 027
content: Maximizing after-tax returns requires strategic asset location, tax-loss harvesting, and vehicle selection tailored to individual circumstances. Client Tax Profile: Client-027 in 35% federal tax bracket, 9.3% California state tax, 20% long-term capital gains rate, 3.8% net investment income tax. Marginal rate on ordinary income: 44.3%, qualified dividends/LTCG: 33.1%. Asset Location Optimization: Tax-deferred accounts (401k, Traditional IRA) - Hold tax-inefficient assets: bonds generating ordinary income, REITs, active funds with high turnover. Roth accounts - Maximize growth potential: small-cap stocks, international equities, investments with highest expected long-term returns never taxed on withdrawal. Taxable accounts - Tax-efficient holdings: index funds with minimal distributions, municipal bonds for tax-free income, growth stocks held long-term for favorable capital gains treatment. Tax-Loss Harvesting: Systematic program captures >$50K annual losses offsetting gains and $3K ordinary income. Losses carried forward indefinitely. Ensure wash sale compliance with 30-day rule. Replace sold positions with similar but not substantially identical securities maintaining market exposure. Municipal Bond Strategy: Current AA-rated municipal yields of 3.8% provide 5.7% taxable-equivalent yield for client-027. California municipal bonds offer state tax exemption increasing efficiency. Ladder maturities from 1-15 years. Roth Conversion Planning: Annual conversions of $80K from traditional IRA staying within 32% bracket. Five-year rule compliance. $2M projected tax-free Roth balance in 15 years. Charitable Giving: Donor-advised fund contributions of appreciated securities avoid capital gains, receive full market value deduction. Qualified charitable distributions from IRA post age 70.5 satisfy RMDs tax-free.
